DANA W. REED

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard 
REED & 
DAVIDSON 
RNEYS AT LAW 
SIXTH STREET, !2'1"f.< FLOCR 
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017 
TELEPHONE {2!3l 362-9238 
FACSiMILE 1213i 623-1692 
September 29, 1989 
ORANGE COUNTY OFFICE 
3151 AIRWAY AVENUE, SUITE M-f 
COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92626 
TELEPHONE (714) 641-:688 
FACSiMILE (714) 546-1003 
Division Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
This letter requests informal assistance under Government Code, 
section 83114(b). 
We request written confirmation of advice this 
firm received from Wayne Imberi on September 25, 1989 concerning 
contributions from a candidate's controlled committee to a ballot 
measure committee controlled by one or more candidates or office-
holders. 
I am requesting this confirmation of advice as a person 
with a duty to advise other persons relating to their duties 
under the Political Reform Act ("the Act"). (2 California Code of 
Regulations, section 18329(c) (1». 
We were advised that a candidate or officeholder committee is not 
prohibited from contributing to a controlled committee primarily 
formed to support or oppose a ballot measure, so long as the 
ballot measure committee does not make contributions to support 
or oppose candidates. 
If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us. 
CD:lb 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Carey Davidson 
Reed & Davidson 
October 10, 1989 
888 West sixth Street, 12th Floor 
Los Angeles, CA 
90017 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-576 
Dear Mr. Davidson: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on October 4, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
f 
I 
'Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660

sections.question: We request written confirmation of advice this firm received from Wayne Imberi on September 25, 1989 concerning contributions from a candidate's controlled committee to a ballot measure committee controlled by one or more candidates or officeholders.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks confirmation of prior informal advice regarding whether a candidate or officeholder committee may contribute to a ballot measure committee controlled by candidates or officeholders, provided the ballot measure committee does not make contributions to support or oppose candidates.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the original request letter (dated September 29, 1989) and the FPPC acknowledgment/intake letter (dated October 10, 1989). The acknowledgment letter indicates this was treated as an informal assistance request under Gov. Code § 83114(b). No formal conclusion or advice has been issued yet - this is the intake stage. The requestor is seeking confirmation of prior oral advice from Wayne Imberi.

embedding.summary: This document contains an incoming request for informal assistance from Reed & Davidson law firm requesting written confirmation of advice previously received from FPPC staff regarding contributions from candidate committees to ballot measure committees, along with the FPPC's acknowledgment letter confirming receipt of the request.
